title: BenchmarkRunCompleted payload
description: Emitted at successful completion of a benchmark run. Carries the closing metrics — per-token-stream-and-wall-clock summary — plus (added 2026-05-20 via T3 of agent-substrate-crud-retirement, §9.6) an optional result_columns block mirroring the rubric-side columns of the benchmark_results CRUD row so payload-only fold reconstruction can rebuild proj_benchmark_results without joining the CRUD table. Per-step MetricRecorded events fire during the run with refs.caused_by_event_id pointing at the corresponding BenchmarkRunStarted; this Completed event marks the run's terminal success state. Provenance is NOT repeated here — it lives on the corresponding BenchmarkRunStarted, joined via refs.caused_by_event_id.

run_id.description: The run_id from the existing benchmark_results schema. Stays as the join key while the legacy benchmark_results table coexists with events.

score.description: Aggregate rubric score for the run. null for runs that didn't produce a scalar score (e.g. completion-only smoke tests).

wall_clock_ms.description: End-to-end wall time in milliseconds. Mirrors the benchmark_results.wall_clock_ms column.

input_tokens.description: Prompt tokens consumed by the run; null if the API didn't expose this.

output_tokens.description: Completion tokens emitted; null if not exposed.

tool_use_tokens.description: Tool-use tokens where available; null otherwise. Mirrors benchmark_results.

result_columns.description: Added 2026-05-20 via T3 of agent-substrate-crud-retirement (§9.6 audit finding). Optional snapshot of the rubric-side columns of the benchmark_results CRUD row this completion produced, so payload-only fold reconstruction can rebuild proj_benchmark_results without joining the CRUD table. Every sub-field mirrors a benchmark_results column verbatim. Optional for backward compatibility with pre-T3 events; once T5 lands, every new event carries this block.

task_id.description: Mirrors benchmark_results.task_id (rubric name for L-tier; scenario_id for E-tier).

benchmark_result_id.description: T5-benchmarks additive bump (2026-05-21): the benchmark_results.id UUID (distinct from run_id) — needed for proj_benchmark_results.id when reconstructing from event alone. Optional; pre-T5 events lack it and the fold falls back to skipping the row.

project_id.description: T5-benchmarks additive bump (2026-05-21): project_id for the row. Cross-cutting events don't carry it in the envelope, so it lands in the payload here.

scenario_id.description: T5-benchmarks additive bump (2026-05-21): scenario_id, lives on the corresponding BenchmarkRunStarted today; mirrored here so the fold doesn't need a cross-event lookup.

provenance_id.description: T5-benchmarks additive bump (2026-05-21): the benchmark_provenance.id FK target.

run_at.description: T5-benchmarks additive bump (2026-05-21): unix-seconds timestamp of the run. The event envelope's ts is an ISO string; the column is INTEGER seconds.
